title: Employee Free Time
description:
Employee Free Time

We are given a list schedule of employees, which represents the working time for each employee.

Each employee has a list of non-overlapping intervals, and these intervals are in sorted order.

Return the list of finite intervals representing the common free time for all employees, also in sorted order.

Even though we represent intervals as [a, b], the intervals within schedule are guaranteed to be non-overlapping for each individual employee.

Examples:

  • Input: schedule = [[[1,2],[5,6]],[[1,3]],[[4,10]]]
    Output: [[3,4]]
    Explanation: There are a total of three employees. All together, the combined working hours are [[1,3],[4,10]]. The free time would be [3,4].
  • Input: schedule = [[[1,3],[6,7]],[[2,4]],[[2,5],[9,12]]]
    Output: [[5,6],[7,9]]
    Explanation: The combined working hours are [[1,5],[6,7],[9,12]]. The free time is [5,6] and [7,9].

Constraints:

  • 1 <= schedule.length <= 50
  • 1 <= schedule[i].length <= 50
  • 0 <= schedule[i][j][0] < schedule[i][j][1] <= 10^8
solution_text:
Employee Free Time

Algorithm:

  1. Flatten all intervals from every employee into one list
  2. Sort by start time
  3. Merge overlapping intervals
  4. Find gaps between merged intervals - these are free times

Time Complexity: O(n log n) where n is the total number of intervals

Space Complexity: O(n)

Key Insights:

  • Individual employee schedules don't matter - flatten everything
  • After merging all work intervals, any gap is a common free time
  • This reduces to "merge intervals" followed by "find gaps"
verify:
def verify(actual_output, expected_output):
    # Normalize to lists of lists for comparison
    actual = [list(x) for x in actual_output] if actual_output else []
    expected = [list(x) for x in expected_output] if expected_output else []
    passed = actual == expected
    return [passed, str(actual)]
